rules.title: Chip must have an accessible label
rules.description: Every chip must have a non-empty accessible name. Satisfied by: visible text inside .chip__label, an aria-label attribute on the root, or aria-labelledby pointing to an existing element.
rules.expected: element has accessible name via text content OR aria-label OR aria-labelledby

conditions: element has non-empty trimmed text content
element has aria-labelledby whose target has non-empty text content

rules.message: Chip has no accessible label. Add visible text inside .chip__label or set aria-label on the chip element.
rules.fix: Add Label text as a child, or set aria-label="Label" on the root element.

rules.title: Focus ring must not be suppressed
rules.description: chip.css defines a visible focus outline via ::after on :focus-visible. Any override that sets outline:none without an equivalent ::after or box-shadow replacement violates PLATFORM-CODE-FOCUS-001.
rules.expected: computed styles include a visible ::after outline — outline:none not present without a ::after replacement
rules.message: Focus ring is suppressed on .chip:focus-visible. Keyboard users cannot see which chip is focused.
rules.fix: Remove the outline:none override or ensure the ::after focus ring defined in chip.css is not overridden.

rules.title: Toggle/selectable chip must declare aria-pressed or aria-selected
rules.description: A chip that represents a persistent on/off or selected state must communicate that state via aria-pressed (standalone toggle) or aria-selected (within a group). The attribute must be 'true' or 'false' — never omitted once the chip is interactive.

check.trigger: element has class is-selected OR has data-toggle attribute

rules.message: Selectable chip is missing aria-pressed or aria-selected. State is not communicated to assistive technology.
rules.fix: Add aria-pressed="false" (standalone toggle) or aria-selected="false" (within role="group") on default. Update to "true" when active.

rules.title: Small chip touch target is below 24px minimum
rules.description: The Small chip size (--chip-sm-height) resolves to 20px, below the 24px internal minimum touch target. Medium (24px) and Large (32px) pass. Small size chips require documented design team exception to ship.
rules.message: Small chip height is 20px, below the 24px minimum. Flagged for design review.
rules.fix: Raise --chip-sm-height to 24px in token.css, or obtain documented design exception acknowledging the 20px value.

rules.title: Interactive chip must use a semantic interactive element
rules.description: The .chip class must be applied only to <button> for interactive chips, or <span>/<div> only when the chip is purely decorative with no click/toggle behavior. Non-interactive elements carrying click handlers require role='button' and tabindex='0'.
rules.expected: element tag is 'button' OR (has no click behavior AND is non-interactive) OR has role='button' and tabindex
rules.message: Interactive chip is rendered as a <{tag}> element without role='button'. Use <button class="chip"> instead.

rules.title: Colors must reference design tokens, not hardcoded values
rules.description: All color values applied to .chip elements must reference CSS custom properties (var(--token-name)). Hardcoded hex, rgb(), or hsl() values bypass the token system and break theme compliance. Note: all chip-scoped tokens are currently pending in token.css — this rule will fail until token.css is updated.
rules.expected: background, color, border-color, outline-color all use var(--) — or are transparent, none, inherit, currentColor
rules.message: Hardcoded color '{value}' found on '{property}'. Replace with the corresponding chip design token.
rules.fix: Replace hardcoded value with the appropriate var(--chip-*) token. See analysis.md §Token Findings for the full token map.

rules.title: Chip must not use physical CSS properties that break RTL
rules.description: Physical directional properties on .chip or its children cause visual defects under dir='rtl'. Use logical properties (padding-inline, margin-inline) instead. The RTL axis in the Figma manifest produces zero CSS differences — RTL is a container-level concern handled by [dir='rtl'].
rules.expected: padding-left, padding-right, margin-left, margin-right, border-left, border-right are absent
rules.message: Physical CSS property '{property}' found on chip. Replace with the logical equivalent.
rules.fix: Replace padding-left/right with padding-inline-start/end. Replace margin-left/right with margin-inline-start/end.

rules.title: Chip label exceeds recommended length
rules.description: Labels longer than 30 characters cause unpredictable text wrapping within the fixed-height container. Chips are compact display elements — labels should be short keywords or phrases. The 30-character limit is a recommendation pending design team confirmation.
rules.expected: text content length is between 1 and 30 characters inclusive
rules.message: Chip label has {count} characters (recommended max 30). Shorten the label.
rules.fix: Reduce the chip label to 30 characters or fewer. If more context is needed, use a tooltip.
rules._note: 30-character limit is a proposal. Pending design team confirmation before this rule is enforced.

severity_legend.error: Blocks release. Must be resolved before component ships to production.
severity_legend.warning: Must be reviewed. Should be resolved; requires documented justification to waive.
severity_legend.info: Advisory. No action required but noted in audit report.

validation_status_legend.not_run: Rule has been defined but not yet executed against this component.
validation_status_legend.pass: Rule was executed and the component passed.
validation_status_legend.fail: Rule was executed and the component failed. See audit report for details.
validation_status_legend.skipped: Rule was intentionally skipped with documented justification.
validation_status_legend.manual_required: Rule cannot be validated automatically and requires human review.
